headline: Spinout Exergy3 raises £10m to decarbonise industrial heat
summary: Exergy3, a University of Edinburgh spinout, raised £10 million (€11.4m) in seed funding to commercialise its thermal energy storage technology that converts excess renewable electricity into high-temperature industrial heat. The round was led by Axeleo Capital's Article 9 Green Tech fund alongside Bayern Kapital and Kibo Invest, with support from Scottish Enterprise, Zero Carbon Capital, and Old College Capital. The company has already piloted its system at Annandale Distillery in southwest Scotland.
raw_snippet: Exergy3, an Edinburgh-based CleanTech startup specialising in ultra-high temperature thermal energy storage solutions, has raised €11.4 million (£10 million) in Seed funding to scale its proprietary technology, addressing industrial decarbonisation, grid balancing and energy security. Lead investors: Axeleo Capital (Article 9 Green Tech Industry I fund), Bayern Capital, Kibo Invest. Follow-on investors: Scottish Enterprise, Zero Carbon Capital, Old College Capital.

investors: Investment Fund for Scotland (Maven Equity Finance / British Business Bank)
lead_investor: Investment Fund for Scotland (Maven Equity Finance / British Business Bank)
headline: Highway Data Systems secures £1.25 million investment from IFS managed by Maven
summary: Glasgow-based Highway Data Systems (HDS), a provider of automated roadside quality assurance technology, has secured £1.25m from the Investment Fund for Scotland managed by Maven. HDS is the first business to secure investment through the Greater Glasgow Innovation Cluster via the IFS. Funds will support new contracts, customer expansion, and continued US operations development.
raw_snippet: Glasgow-based Highway Data Systems (HDS), a provider of automated roadside quality assurance, has secured a £1.25 million investment from the British Business Bank's Investment Fund for Scotland, through Fund Manager, Maven. HDS is the first business to secure investment through the Greater Glasgow Innovation Cluster via the Investment Fund for Scotland.

headline: Cnuic secures $3m pre-seed to unlock next-generation photonic chip production
summary: Edinburgh-based Cnuic raised $3m in an oversubscribed pre-seed round led by Tensor Ventures to develop a new photolithography device that uses light rather than electrons to enable fast, reconfigurable production of photonic chips. The technology is intended to democratise access to photonic chip manufacturing, with potential implications for European semiconductor sovereignty. Co-founder Omar Durrani described it as 'building the tools that make that possible at scale.'
raw_snippet: Cnuic, headquartered in Edinburgh, raised $3m in pre-seed funding led by Tensor Ventures and Blank Space Ventures, with Silicon Roundabout Ventures, Phasechange, SANDS, and Superlative also participating. The company has built a working prototype of a new photolithography device using light for fast, reconfigurable chip production. Co-founder Omar Durrani: 'We learned to use electrons. Now we are learning to use light.' Tensor Ventures' Martin Drdúl: 'This is a completely new technology and a major breakthrough.' Announced April 28, 2026.

headline: Scottish quantum hardware company raises £2.5m to build UK's sovereign manufacturing capability
summary: Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spin-out, raised £2.5m in seed funding to create a sovereign supply chain for quantum hardware as the UK races to build domestic capacity in critical technologies. The round was co-led by PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures and Scottish Enterprise, with investment also from Quantum Exponential and STAC. Quantcore plans to grow its team from four to 12 employees over the next 18 months.
raw_snippet: Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spin-out, has raised £2.5m in seed funding to create a sovereign supply chain for quantum hardware, as the UK races to build domestic capacity in technologies critical to national security and economic competitiveness. The round was co-led by PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures and Scottish Enterprise, with investment also coming from Quantum Exponential and STAC.

headline: BIOCAPTIVA secures £1.58M to transform how blood samples are prepared for liquid biopsy
summary: BIOCAPTIVA, headquartered at Roslin Innovation Centre in Midlothian, raised £1.58 million led by Archangels. The funding will support R&D to expand its msX magnetic bead platform — which extracts cell-free DNA from whole blood without centrifuges — and has enabled the company to launch its first product in the US market.
raw_snippet: The raise was led by existing investor Archangels, with support from Old College Capital, BBI, Scottish Enterprise, and new investor EverQuest Capital Partners... BIOCAPTIVA has raised £1.58 million in a new funding round and launched its first product in the US.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Daily Business)
headline: SWURF raises £200k to expand platform
summary: Edinburgh-based SWURF, a remote-working platform connecting workers to on-demand private meeting pods, raised £200k in March 2026 from existing angel backers including Skyscanner co-founder Gareth Williams, alongside new unnamed high-net-worth investors. The funding will accelerate rollout of SWURF Pods across Edinburgh with the goal of making Edinburgh the world's most flexible working-friendly city.
raw_snippet: SWURF secured £200,000 of investment on March 9, 2026, to accelerate expansion. Existing backers include Gareth Williams (Skyscanner co-founder) and Anna Lagerqvist Christopherson (owner of Boda BV, Green Room and Victoria Bar). New investors are unnamed high-net-worth individuals. Platform currently lists over 450 venues and serves approximately 14,000 users. Board chaired by Alison Grieve (G-Hold founder).

investors: Maven Capital Partners (Investment Fund for Scotland)
headline: Ultrasonic surgery specialist Nami appoints Nikki Palfrey as CEO and completes latest investment round
summary: Nami Surgical, a University of Glasgow spinout developing ultrasonic robotic-assisted surgery technology, completed a £1.9m round led by Eos Advisory with participation from Maven and Scottish Enterprise. The funding supports the company's transition from advanced development to commercialisation in the robotic-assisted surgery market. New CEO Nikki Palfrey was appointed alongside the close.
raw_snippet: Nami recently completed its latest investment, a £1.9 million round led by Eos Advisory with participation from the Investment Fund for Scotland managed by Maven Capital Partners (and delivered by the British Business Bank), and Scottish Enterprise. The funding supports the company's transition from advanced development to commercialisation as Nami targets the global robotic-assisted surgery market, which is forecast to reach over $25 billion by 2030.

investors: Investment Fund for Scotland (IFS Maven Equity Finance)
headline: Maven leads £2.6 million funding round in Esk
summary: Edinburgh-based Esk, which delivers high-end live experiences for blue-chip brands including Netflix, Paramount, and BAFTA, secured £2.6m from Maven Capital Partners via its VCTs and the Investment Fund for Scotland. The funding will support business development, marketing, IP portfolio growth, and development of the company's proprietary hardware technology. Esk has completed over 550 shows globally.
raw_snippet: Maven leads £2.6m investment in Esk, an Edinburgh-based global entertainment technology company that delivers high-end live experiences for brands such as Netflix, Paramount and BAFTA. Funding from Maven Income and Growth VCTs and the Investment Fund for Scotland (IFS Maven Equity Finance), delivered by the British Business Bank. The capital will enable Esk to invest in business development, marketing, IP portfolio expansion, and proprietary hardware development. Announced May 11, 2026.

headline: STAC backs three Scottish deeptech companies with £772k
summary: Glasgow-based Vuabl raised £222k from the STAC Invest syndicate as part of a £772k cohort investment in three companies completing STAC's 18-month accelerator programme. Vuabl has built software using the LIDAR sensors in modern smartphones to scan properties and generate comprehensive condition reports, supporting housing providers meeting new energy efficiency and tenant safety regulations.
raw_snippet: STAC deployed £772,000 across three Glasgow-based deeptech ventures: Quantcore Technologies (£250,000), Vuabl (£222,000), and Airspection (£300,000). All three completed STAC's 18-month accelerator programme. Vuabl uses smartphone LIDAR sensors for property condition reports and EPC ratings. Announced April 1, 2026.

headline: STAC backs three Scottish deeptech companies with £772k
summary: Glasgow-based Airspection raised £300k from the STAC Invest syndicate as part of a £772k cohort investment in three companies completing STAC's 18-month accelerator programme. Airspection builds autonomous drone technology for offshore wind turbine inspections, flying from safe locations to remote infrastructure in extreme conditions to enhance safety and reduce environmental impact.
raw_snippet: STAC deployed £772,000 across three Glasgow-based deeptech ventures: Quantcore Technologies (£250,000), Vuabl (£222,000), and Airspection (£300,000). All three completed STAC's 18-month accelerator programme. Airspection's drones fly to remote locations for offshore wind, power lines, rail networks, and coastal assets. Announced April 1, 2026.

headline: Bioliberty Announces $10.5 million Series A Financing to Build Functional Intelligence Capabilities for Post-Acute Care
summary: Edinburgh- and Boston-based Bioliberty raised $10.5 million in a Series A led by the Scottish National Investment Bank. The funds will expand the company's Lifehub Clinic platform — which uses AI and robotics to monitor functional recovery in post-acute care settings — and commercialise Lifehub Home for in-home physical therapy tracking. The round brings total funding to $17.5 million.
raw_snippet: Bioliberty is an Edinburgh- and Boston-based startup... Participating in the round were new investors including lead investor The Scottish National Investment Bank and Conduit Connect. Returning investors Archangel Investors, Eos Advisory, Hanna Capital, and Old College Capital also participated.

headline: Earth Blox secures £6m to finance the transition to a nature-positive economy
summary: Edinburgh-based Earth Blox secured £6 million led by PXN Ventures to help organisations understand how nature loss and climate hazards affect financial performance. The round also includes follow-on investment from Archangels, support from Scottish Enterprise, and funding from the European Space Agency. The platform combines satellite, environmental, and portfolio data with AI-powered analysis for global banks and businesses.
raw_snippet: Earth Blox, an Edinburgh-based technology company helping organisations understand how nature loss and climate hazards affect financial performance, has secured £6 million in new investment led by PXN Ventures with support from Scottish Enterprise, follow-on investment from Archangels and funding from the European Space Agency (ESA).

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via ADBA press release)
headline: $3 Million Investment for Edinburgh-Based Specialist Bio-Carbon Designer Carbogenics
summary: Carbogenics, a University of Edinburgh spinout founded in 2016, closed a $3m funding round on January 19, 2026 to accelerate development and international expansion of its CreChar biochar product. Equity investors include Dangerous Ventures (US), Green Angel Ventures (UK), and Old College Capital; grant funding from Innovate UK, New Mexico Gas, and New Mexico Government Economic Development Department also contributed. The company plans US manufacturing expansion alongside scaled UK operations.
raw_snippet: Carbogenics closed $3 million in investment and grant funding on January 19, 2026. Equity investors: Dangerous Ventures, Green Angel Ventures, Old College Capital (University of Edinburgh venture fund), Scottish Enterprise. Grant bodies: Innovate UK, New Mexico Gas, New Mexico Government Economic Development Department. Funds accelerate development of CreChar biochar for the biogas industry and support US manufacturing expansion.

headline: GovTech startup ePass rolls out nationwide platform after £1m debut year
summary: Edinburgh-based ePass, founded in 2024 by James Buchan and Chris Renga following a CivTech competition win, achieved £1m revenue in its first twelve months and deployed its licensing platform across all 32 Scottish local authorities serving over 15,000 businesses. The company is actively engaging with VC and investor communities and is likely to pursue its first external fundraising round later in 2026. This is not yet a closed investment round.
raw_snippet: GovTech startup ePass generated £1m revenue in its debut year and rolled out its platform nationwide across Scotland's 32 local authorities. Co-founders James Buchan (CEO) and Chris Renga (CTO) note the company is 'actively engaging with venture capital and investor communities' and is 'likely to embark on its first external fundraising round later this year.' No external VC investment has yet been closed as of announcement date May 25, 2026.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Scottish Financial Review)
headline: EnteroBiotix, Bellshill biopharma, raises £19m
summary: EnteroBiotix, a Bellshill-based biopharmaceutical company focused on microbiome therapeutics, raised £19m in growth financing led by Thairm Bio, with participation from the Scottish National Investment Bank. The funds will advance lead candidate EBX-102-02 into Phase 2b development for irritable bowel syndrome with constipation, following a positive Phase 2a trial. First patient dosing is expected Q2 2026 with topline data anticipated H2 2027.
raw_snippet: EnteroBiotix secured £19m in growth financing led by Thairm Bio and the Scottish National Investment Bank. Following the positive Phase 2a TrIuMPH trial outcome demonstrating efficacy of EBX-102-02 over placebo in IBS patients, the company will commence the largest ever full-spectrum microbiome therapeutic trial in IBS. CEO James McIlroy confirmed first patient dosing expected Q2 2026 and topline efficacy data anticipated H2 2027. Announced April 29, 2026.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Scottish Financial Review)
headline: Entourage AI, Glasgow uni health spinout, in $5m raise
summary: Entourage AI, a University of Glasgow spinout focused on longevity science and AI-powered health data analysis, raised $5m in a pre-seed round led by Provision. The company opened a Research and Operations Centre at Glasgow's West of Scotland Science Park, with Scottish Government Business Minister Richard Lochhead attending. The company was founded by Professor Patrick T. Gunning and aims to transform how human healthspan is measured and extended.
raw_snippet: Entourage AI secured $5 million in pre-seed funding led by Provision, with participation from GU Holdings Ltd (University of Glasgow's investment arm), David Peterson, Brad Peltz, and angel investors. The University of Glasgow spinout opened a Research and Operations Centre in Glasgow's West of Scotland Science Park. Founded by serial entrepreneur Professor Patrick T. Gunning, the company focuses on longevity science using AI to transform how human health is measured. Announced March 18, 2026.

source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Glasgow City of Science and Innovation)
headline: Glasgow healthtech spinout raises £1.6m to transform cancer diagnosis with AI
summary: TileBio, a University of Glasgow spinout, raised £1.6m in seed funding led by Twin Path Ventures to commercialise AI technology capable of learning the 'language of cancer' from unlabelled tissue images. The company's self-learning models bypass the need for manual data annotation — a key bottleneck in digital pathology AI — and will use the funding to accelerate commercialisation and clinical deployment of its diagnostic platform.
raw_snippet: TileBio secured £1.6m in seed funding led by Twin Path Ventures, with participation from Scottish Enterprise and GU Holdings Ltd. CEO Dr Christopher Walsh: 'We have developed a method that allows AI systems to interpret the language of tissue directly from millions of unlabelled images.' Twin Path Ventures' Katie Lockwood: 'Their models completely bypass the industry's biggest bottleneck: the need for manual data annotation.' Announced March 3, 2026.

headline: Edinburgh AI ecommerce firm raises over £1m in new investment
summary: Edinburgh-based stampfree.ai, which develops AI-powered parcel shipping and returns technology, raised just over £1m in a new round led by long-term backer R42 Group alongside crowdfunding investors on Crowdcube and Republic. The company's innovations include WhatsApp-based returns management and patented label-free shipping technology deployed with Royal Mail, Evri, and QazPost in Kazakhstan. CEO Hugh Craigie Halkett described the round as oversubscribed.
raw_snippet: stampfree.ai raised £1,029,968 in a new investment round as of May 20, 2026. R42 Group is described as a 'long-term investor' providing 'continued backing' across all fundraising rounds. Additional investors include the Crowdcube and Republic crowdfunding communities plus unnamed existing and new investors. CEO Hugh Craigie Halkett: 'This latest investment round was once again hugely oversubscribed, reflecting the confidence both of existing and new investors.' The company has secured EU and US patents for its label-free technology.

headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
summary: Scottish angel syndicate Tricapital invested £506,000 in HonuWorx, a subsea robotics specialist. The deal forms part of a batch of investments totalling more than £3 million by Tricapital, whose members have invested £6.8m in Scottish businesses over two and a half years, matched by Scottish Enterprise. HonuWorx recently secured its first NATO contract through the defence innovation accelerator for the North Atlantic programme.
raw_snippet: The latest investments involve £506,000 of backing for subsea robotics specialist HonuWorx... Angel syndicate Tricapital has led a series of fresh investments totalling more than £3 million as it focuses on critical areas such as the industrial transition and ocean technology.

headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
summary: Tricapital invested £430,000 in Kaly, a sustainable seaweed business expanding its island-based seaweed supply chain. The investment is part of Tricapital's wider £3m-plus investment batch focused on the industrial transition and ocean technology sectors in Scotland.
raw_snippet: £430,000 for sustainable seaweed business Kaly... Kaly expands its island-based seaweed supply chain.

headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
summary: Tricapital invested £620,000 in Sisaltech, a low-carbon insulation manufacturer scaling production of natural fibre insulation products. The deal is part of Tricapital's latest batch of investments totalling over £3 million in early-stage Scottish businesses.
raw_snippet: £620,000 for low-carbon insulation manufacturer Sisaltech... Sisaltech continues to scale production of natural fibre insulation products.

headline: TRICAPITAL Angels leads £1.2m investment into sovereign dual use 5G solutions
summary: JET Connectivity, a UK-developed technology company delivering ultra-reliable, infrastructure-independent 5G connectivity to hard-to-reach locations, secured a £1.2m investment led by Tricapital Angels. The funding will accelerate growth at a time of increasing global focus on national resilience and secure communications infrastructure. JET Connectivity provides land and sea-based alternatives to satellite networks for offshore, coastal and ultra-remote sites.
raw_snippet: A pioneering UK-developed technology delivering ultra-reliable, infrastructure-independent 5G connectivity to the world's hardest-to-reach locations has secured a £1.2million investment to accelerate its growth. JET Connectivity has developed land and sea-based alternatives to satellite networks such as Starlink and brings secure, high-bandwidth communications to offshore, coastal and ultra-remote sites anywhere on the planet.

headline: Lentitek raises £700k for cancer treatment technology
summary: Edinburgh biotech Lentitek secured £700,000 from Equity Gap, the Scottish angel syndicate, to accelerate its next-generation cancer treatments. The round brings total investment to £1m in the last six months, following a £300,000 Innovate UK grant. Funding was also provided by Gabriel-IS, Apollo Informal Investment, Scottish Enterprise and prominent private investors.
raw_snippet: Biotech company Lentitek has secured £700,000 from Equity Gap, the Scottish angel syndicate, to accelerate its next-generation cancer treatments. It brings total investment in the Edinburgh business to £1 million in the last six months, following a £300,000 Innovate UK grant.

headline: Launchpad announces PXN Ventures as its newest strategic investor
summary: Launchpad, an AI-first robotics company powering real-world assembly automation, announced PXN Ventures as a new strategic investor, adding to its oversubscribed $11m Series A fundraising announced in October 2025. PXN Ventures forms part of PXN Group with £700m AUM across over 120 portfolio companies following its launch in November 2025 after the merger of Par Equity and Praetura Ventures.
raw_snippet: Launchpad, the AI-first robotics company powering real-world assembly automation, today announced PXN Ventures as a new investor. This builds on the company's oversubscribed, $11M Series A fundraising, announced in October 2025.
